construction_checks.py¶

Construction feasibility checks for HP-shell slab constructions Numbering according to Loutfi [3]

Verifies that the chosen geometry and reinforcement layout are constructable: sufficient concrete cover, adequate bar spacing, and minimum shell thickness.

Adapted from: Jamila Loutfi [3]

class ConstructionCheck[Quellcode]¶

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for construction feasibility checks. Author: Elliot Melcer

All ConstructionCheck subclasses must implement calculate_utilization(), which returns a dimensionless utilization ratio where values ≤ 1.0 indicate a constructable design.

abstractmethod static calculate_utilization(slab_construction: SlabConstruction) → float[Quellcode]¶

Compute the construction utilization ratio for a slab construction.

Parameter:

slab_construction (SlabConstruction) – Full slab construction object.

Rückgabe:

Utilization ratio [-]. Values ≤ 1.0 indicate a passing design.

Rückgabetyp:

float

class MidlineConcreteCoverCheck[Quellcode]¶

Bases: ConstructionCheck

Concrete cover check along the HP-shell midline (C.1).

Verifies that the available clear concrete cover c_1 satisfies the minimum requirement c_nom,req = 3 · d_p, where d_p is the equivalent bar diameter of the outermost reinforcement layer.

A utilization ≤ 1.0 means the concrete cover is sufficient. Infeasible designs (c_1 = 0) are penalized with a fixed utilization of 10.0.

static calculate_utilization(slab_construction: SlabConstruction) → float[Quellcode]¶

Compute the concrete cover utilization ratio c_nom,req / c_1.

Parameter:

slab_construction (SlabConstruction) – Full slab construction object.

Rückgabe:

Utilization ratio c_nom,req / c_1 [-]. Returns 10.0 if the available cover is zero (infeasible design).

Rückgabetyp:

float

class ReinforcementSpacingCheck[Quellcode]¶

Bases: ConstructionCheck

Clear reinforcement spacing check along the HP-shell midline (C.2).

Verifies that the minimum available spacing s_min between reinforcement bars satisfies s_req = 5.75 · d_p.

A utilization ≤ 1.0 means the spacing is sufficient. Infeasible designs (s_min = 0) are penalized with a fixed utilization of 10.0.

static calculate_utilization(slab_construction: SlabConstruction, debug_print: bool = False) → float[Quellcode]¶

Compute the reinforcement spacing utilization ratio s_req / s_min.

Parameter:

  • slab_construction (SlabConstruction) – Full slab construction object.

  • debug_print (bool, optional) – If True, prints the minimum available clear spacing to stdout. Default is False.

Rückgabe:

Utilization ratio s_req / s_min [-]. Returns 10.0 if the minimum spacing is zero (infeasible design).

Rückgabetyp:

float

class MinimumHPShellThicknessCheck[Quellcode]¶

Bases: ConstructionCheck

Minimum HP-shell thickness check (C.3).

Derives the required shell thickness from the combined C.1 and C.2 requirements:

A utilization ≤ 1.0 means the shell is thick enough to accommodate the reinforcement with the required cover and spacing.

static calculate_utilization(slab_construction: SlabConstruction) → float[Quellcode]¶

Compute the shell thickness utilization ratio t_req / t.

Parameter:

slab_construction (SlabConstruction) – Full slab construction object.

Rückgabe:

Utilization ratio t_req / t [-].

Rückgabetyp:

float
